# UX Overhaul Design Spec — Mobile & Desktop

**Author:** designer | **Date:** 2026-03-11 | **Status:** Ready for builder

## Problem Statement

The user's feedback is clear and accurate:

**Mobile**: The sidebar consumes the entire screen. The map — which IS the product — is secondary, crammed into 45vh. There is no way to dismiss controls and just browse the map. On a phone, the current layout is essentially a list with a map thumbnail.

**Desktop**: The sidebar order buries the most valuable content. The world overview (a one-sentence global summary generated by the LLM) sits below search, worlds, AND filters. Search is prominent despite being rudimentary. The time/opinion/filter controls are jammed together. The worlds bar can overflow when 4+ pills exist. The whole sidebar hasn't been rethought since v1 — it has grown organically and it shows.

**Core principle reminder**: The map IS the product. Everything else supports map exploration. This means the map gets 100% of viewport by default on mobile, and the sidebar exists to *enhance* the map, not compete with it.

---

## Part 1: Mobile Redesign

### 1.1 Default State: Map Fills the Viewport

When a mobile user lands on thisminute.org, they see:

```
+--------------------------------------------------+
|                                                    |
|                                                    |
|                    FULL MAP                         |
|                 (100% viewport)                    |
|                                                    |
|                                                    |
|                          [Space] [Internet]   (TR) |
|                                                    |
|                                                    |
| [globe]                                            |
|                                                    |
+--------------------------------------------------+
| [stats pill]          [hamburger icon]        (FB) |
+--------------------------------------------------+
```

- The map takes **100vh** minus a thin floating bar at the bottom
- No sidebar visible at all on load
- Space/Internet buttons remain in top-right corner (existing position)
- Globe toggle remains as-is (bottom-left area above the floating bar)
- A **floating bottom bar** (56px tall) always visible, containing:
  - Left: stats pill showing "3,421 stories" (tappable to expand)
  - Center: active world name + color indicator (e.g., blue dot + "News")
  - Right: hamburger/menu icon to open the bottom sheet

### 1.2 Bottom Sheet Panel

Tapping the hamburger icon (or swiping up from the floating bar) opens a **bottom sheet** that slides up from the bottom of the screen. This is the standard modern mobile pattern (Google Maps, Apple Maps, Uber, etc.).

```
+--------------------------------------------------+
|                                                    |
|              MAP (dimmed/visible behind)           |
|                                                    |
+==================================================+
| ─── (drag handle) ───                              |
| thisminute                    [sun] [?] [X]        |
+--------------------------------------------------+
| ▾ "Global tensions rise as..." (world overview)    |
+--------------------------------------------------+
| [NEWS] [SPORTS] [ENTERTAIN] [POSITIVE] [+]        |
+--------------------------------------------------+
| [Situations] [Events]                              |
+--------------------------------------------------+
|                                                    |
|   (scrollable situation/event list)                |
|                                                    |
|                                                    |
+--------------------------------------------------+
```

#### Bottom sheet behaviors:

1. **Three snap points:**
   - **Closed** (0%): Only the floating bar visible. Map is fully interactive.
   - **Half** (~50vh): Bottom sheet covers bottom half. Map visible and interactive above it. This is the default open position.
   - **Full** (~90vh): Bottom sheet nearly full screen. Map peeking at top (10vh). For deep browsing.

2. **Swipe gestures:**
   - Swipe up on floating bar: opens to half position
   - Swipe up on sheet at half: expands to full
   - Swipe down on sheet: collapses one step (full -> half -> closed)
   - Swipe down on drag handle: always closes fully

3. **Drag handle:** A small pill-shaped handle at the top of the sheet (standard iOS/Android pattern). CSS-only, 36px wide, 4px tall, centered, rounded, subtle gray.

4. **Content order inside the sheet** (top to bottom, reflecting importance):
   1. Drag handle
   2. Header row: "thisminute" + theme toggle + shortcuts + close button
   3. World overview text (collapsible, 2-line clamp with tap-to-expand)
   4. Worlds bar (horizontal scroll)
   5. View toggles (Situations | Events)
   6. Scrollable content (situation list or event list)
   7. Filter access: a "Filters" FAB (floating action button) pinned to bottom-right of the sheet, or an expandable filter section below the view toggles

5. **Search and filters:** These are secondary on mobile. They live inside an expandable section within the bottom sheet. The "Filters" button expands a collapsible section containing:
   - Search box
   - Time filter
   - Opinion checkbox
   - Source/topic filters (the existing drawer content)
   - This keeps them accessible but not taking space by default

6. **Info panel on mobile:** When a user taps a story/situation/event, the info panel slides up as a *replacement* bottom sheet (same position, same drag behavior). A back arrow in the header returns to the main bottom sheet. This replaces the current separate full-width bottom sheet that has no connection to the main panel.

### 1.3 Mobile CSS Structure

**Breakpoint:** `@media (max-width: 768px)` (keep existing breakpoint)

Key structural changes:
```
body {
    flex-direction: column;  /* keep */
}

#sidebar {
    /* REMOVE: width: 100%, max-height: 55vh, order: 2 */
    /* ADD: */
    position: fixed;
    bottom: 0;
    left: 0;
    right: 0;
    z-index: 1100;
    max-height: 90vh;
    border-radius: 16px 16px 0 0;
    transform: translateY(calc(100% - 56px));  /* only floating bar visible */
    transition: transform 0.3s cubic-bezier(0.4, 0, 0.2, 1);
    overflow: hidden;
}

#sidebar.sheet-half {
    transform: translateY(50%);
}

#sidebar.sheet-full {
    transform: translateY(10vh);
}

#map-container {
    /* REMOVE: height: 45vh, order: 1 */
    /* ADD: */
    height: 100vh;
    width: 100vw;
}
```

### 1.4 Floating Bar (New Element)

A new element `#mobile-bar` appears only on mobile. It lives at the top of `#sidebar` (so it moves with the sidebar but is always visible when the sheet is "closed"):

```html
<div id="mobile-bar">
    <span class="mobile-bar-stats">3,421 stories</span>
    <span class="mobile-bar-world">
        <span class="mobile-bar-world-dot" style="background: #1f6feb"></span>
        News
    </span>
    <button id="mobile-bar-menu" aria-label="Open menu">
        <svg><!-- 3-line hamburger --></svg>
    </button>
</div>
```

On desktop, `#mobile-bar { display: none; }`.
On mobile, it becomes the persistent bottom strip.

### 1.5 Mobile Interaction Flows

**Flow: Browse casually**
```
User lands -> full map -> taps a dot on map -> popup shows story title
-> taps "Read more" in popup -> info panel slides up as bottom sheet
-> swipes down -> back to full map
```

**Flow: Explore situations**
```
User taps hamburger -> bottom sheet opens to half
-> sees world overview + situations list
-> scrolls situations -> taps one -> info panel replaces bottom sheet
-> reads stories -> taps back arrow -> returns to situations list
-> swipes down twice -> sheet closes, full map restored
```

**Flow: Apply filters**
```
User opens bottom sheet -> taps "Filters" button
-> filter section expands (search, time, topics, sources)
-> selects "Sports" world -> sheet stays open, map updates behind it
-> swipes down -> enjoys filtered map
```

---

## Part 2: Desktop Redesign

### 2.1 New Sidebar Order

The current order is: header > stats > search > worlds > time/opinion/filters > world overview > view toggles > content.

The new order prioritizes information density and puts the most useful content first:

```
+------------------------------------------+
| thisminute                    [sun] [?]   |  <- header (unchanged)
+------------------------------------------+
| 3,421 stories · 52 sources · 847 shown   |  <- stats bar (unchanged)
+------------------------------------------+
| ▾ "Rising tensions dominate as three     |  <- MOVED UP: world overview
|   major conflicts intensify..."           |     (most informative element)
+------------------------------------------+
| [NEWS] [SPORTS] [ENTERTAIN] [POSITIVE] +]|  <- worlds bar (unchanged)
+------------------------------------------+
| [Situations] [Events]                     |  <- view toggles (MOVED UP)
+------------------------------------------+
|                                           |
|   (scrollable situation/event list)       |  <- main content
|                                           |
+------------------------------------------+
| ─── FILTER STRIP ──────────────────────── |  <- NEW: collapsed filter strip
| Last 24h ▾ | Filters (3) | [Search icon] |     at the BOTTOM of sidebar
+------------------------------------------+
```

**Rationale for each move:**

1. **World overview moves up** (from position 6 to position 3): This is the most informative text on the page — a one-sentence summary of the current global state. Burying it below search, worlds, and filters meant most users never saw it. It should be the first thing after the stats bar.

2. **View toggles move up** (from position 7 to position 5): The situations/events list is the primary browsing mechanism. Getting to it faster is better.

3. **Search, time, and opinion filters move to the bottom**: These are secondary tools. Power users will find them; casual users won't be confused by them. The search that "doesn't even really work" should not be the most prominent element in the sidebar.

4. **Filter drawer stays as-is** but is triggered from the bottom strip, expanding upward.

### 2.2 World Overview Enhancement

Currently the world overview is a truncated 2-line text that is invisible on mobile and buried on desktop. In the new layout:

- Placed directly below stats bar (position 3)
- Shows 2 lines by default with `-webkit-line-clamp: 2`
- Click/tap to expand to full text
- When filters are active, text dims to 50% opacity (existing behavior, keep)
- On mobile bottom sheet, this appears right below the header

### 2.3 Bottom Filter Strip (New Desktop Pattern)

Instead of the current inline filter row (time/opinion/filters all crammed between worlds and overview), create a **filter strip** pinned to the bottom of the sidebar:

```
+------------------------------------------+
| Last 24h ▾ | Filters (3)       [Q icon]  |
+------------------------------------------+
```

Components:
- **Time dropdown**: Same `<select>` element, just repositioned
- **Filters button**: Shows count of active filters in parentheses. Opens the filter drawer as a *flyout panel* upward from the strip (existing drawer behavior)
- **Search icon**: A magnifying glass icon that, when clicked, expands into the search input field. This replaces the always-visible search box that takes up an entire row. When not focused, it collapses back to just the icon.
  - The placeholder text changes to: "Search by keyword, actor, or location..."
  - This communicates what the search does, addressing the user's complaint

**Hide opinion checkbox**: Move into the filter drawer. It is a filter, not a primary control. Placing it in the main filter drawer with source and topic chips is more logical.

### 2.4 Worlds Bar Overflow Fix

The current worlds bar uses `display: flex` with `gap: 6px` and no wrapping. With 4 built-in worlds (News, Sports, Entertainment, Positive) plus the [+] button, it overflows on narrow sidebars.

**Fix:**
- Add `flex-wrap: wrap` on desktop (the mobile version already uses `overflow-x: auto` which is correct for horizontal scrolling)
- When wrapping occurs, gap between rows should be `4px`
- Alternatively (preferred): make world pill text smaller when 4+ pills exist. Currently `font-size: 11px` with `padding: 5px 16px`. With 4 pills:
  - Reduce padding to `5px 12px`
  - Keep font-size at `11px`
  - This saves ~32px total (8px per pill), enough to fit 4 pills + the [+] button in 360px

**Calculation:** 4 pills at `11px` font with `5px 12px` padding ~ 4 * 75px = 300px. Plus [+] button at 28px. Plus 5 gaps at 6px = 30px. Total ~358px. Fits in 360px sidebar.

Actually, the simpler and more future-proof fix: add `overflow-x: auto` on desktop too, matching the mobile pattern. This way 5, 6, or more worlds all work without wrapping. Add a subtle fade/gradient on the right edge to indicate scrollability.

### 2.5 Search UX Improvement

Current: `<input placeholder="Search stories...">`
- Doesn't explain what it searches
- Always visible despite being secondary functionality
- Occupies a full row

New approach:
1. **Icon-triggered expansion**: In the bottom filter strip, a magnifying glass icon sits on the right. Clicking it expands an input field with a smooth animation.
2. **Better placeholder**: "Search by keyword, actor, or location..."
3. **Collapse on blur**: When the search box loses focus and is empty, it collapses back to just the icon.
4. **Active state**: When search has text, the icon stays blue and shows a small "x" to clear.

This is a well-established pattern (e.g., GitHub, YouTube, many native apps).

### 2.6 Sidebar Width

Keep at 360px. The current width is appropriate for the content density. Making it narrower would compromise readability of situation titles and story summaries. Making it wider steals map space.

However, the sidebar could benefit from more internal breathing room — the content goes edge-to-edge at `padding: 6px 20px` for most sections. This is acceptable.

### 2.7 Desktop Visual Hierarchy Diagram

```
+============================================+
| thisminute                     [sun] [?]    |  HEADER (18px)
+---------------------------------------------+
| 3,421 stories · 52 sources · 847 shown      |  STATS (11px, subtle)
+---------------------------------------------+
| WORLD  "Rising tensions dominate as three    |  WORLD OVERVIEW (11px,
|        major conflicts intensify across..."  |  important, 2-line clamp)
+---------------------------------------------+
| [NEWS] [SPORTS] [ENTERTAIN.] [POSITIVE] [+] |  WORLDS BAR (pill buttons)
+---------------------------------------------+
| [  SITUATIONS  ] [  EVENTS  ]               |  VIEW TOGGLES
+---------------------------------------------+
|                                              |
|  Ukraine-Russia Conflict         [47]        |  SCROLLABLE CONTENT
|  ▸ Latest escalation in...                   |  (situations or events)
|                                              |
|  Middle East Crisis              [32]        |
|  ▸ Ongoing developments...                   |
|                                              |
|  Climate Summit 2026             [18]        |
|  ▸ Key negotiations...                       |
|                                              |
|  (... more items ...)                        |
|                                              |
|                                              |
+---------------------------------------------+
| Last 24h ▾ | Filters (3)        [Q icon]    |  FILTER STRIP (bottom-pinned)
+=============================================+
```

---

## Part 3: Responsive Breakpoint Strategy

### 3.1 Breakpoints

| Breakpoint | Device class | Layout |
|------------|-------------|--------|
| `>= 769px` | Desktop/tablet landscape | Sidebar (360px) + map (flex: 1), side-by-side |
| `<= 768px` | Phone/tablet portrait | Full-screen map + bottom sheet sidebar |

Keep the single breakpoint at 768px. Adding more breakpoints creates exponentially more testing surface with diminishing returns. The bottom sheet pattern works well from 320px to 768px phone widths.

### 3.2 Tablet Considerations

Tablets in landscape (>768px) get the desktop layout. Tablets in portrait (<=768px) get the mobile layout. This is correct — a 768px-wide iPad in portrait has too little horizontal space for a 360px sidebar plus a usable map.

---

## Part 4: Shared Improvements (Both Layouts)

### 4.1 World Overview Behavior

- **Default**: 2-line clamp with ellipsis
- **Interaction**: Click/tap to expand. Click again (or click elsewhere) to collapse.
- **Visual**: A subtle "WORLD" label on the left (existing), text to the right (existing)
- **When filters active**: Text dims to 50% opacity with a note "(filtered)" to indicate the overview reflects all stories, not the filtered set
- **On mobile**: This is the first content item in the bottom sheet (below the header)

### 4.2 Info Panel (Right Panel)

No changes to the info panel's function. However, on mobile, the info panel should use the same bottom-sheet presentation as the sidebar, replacing it rather than overlapping. A back button in the info panel header returns to the sidebar sheet.

On desktop, the info panel remains a fixed right-side panel at 340px width. No changes.

### 4.3 Filter Drawer Expansion Direction

On desktop, the filter drawer currently expands downward from the filter row, pushing the view toggles and content down. In the new layout, with the filter strip at the bottom, the drawer should expand **upward** from the strip, overlaying the situation/event list. This prevents the content from jumping.

Implementation: The filter drawer becomes `position: absolute; bottom: 100%; left: 0; right: 0;` relative to the filter strip container. It gets a max-height and scroll.

### 4.4 Space/Internet Buttons

No layout changes. They remain floating over the map in the top-right corner. The directive mentions making them larger at globe zoom — that is a separate task and out of scope for this layout redesign.

---

## Part 5: Implementation Plan

### 5.1 HTML Changes

1. **Add `#mobile-bar`** inside `#sidebar` as the first child:
   ```html
   <div id="mobile-bar">
       <span class="mobile-bar-stats"></span>
       <span class="mobile-bar-world"></span>
       <button id="mobile-bar-menu" aria-label="Open menu">&#9776;</button>
   </div>
   ```

2. **Add `#drag-handle`** inside `#sidebar`, after `#mobile-bar`:
   ```html
   <div id="drag-handle"><span></span></div>
   ```

3. **Reorder sidebar children** to match the new hierarchy:
   ```html
   <div id="sidebar">
       <!-- Mobile only -->
       <div id="mobile-bar">...</div>
       <div id="drag-handle"><span></span></div>

       <!-- Shared (both layouts) -->
       <div id="sidebar-header">...</div>
       <div id="stats-bar">...</div>
       <div id="world-overview-bar">...</div>       <!-- MOVED UP from position 6 -->
       <div id="worlds-bar">...</div>
       <div id="view-toggles">...</div>              <!-- MOVED UP from position 7 -->

       <!-- Scrollable content -->
       <div id="narrative-list">...</div>
       <div id="event-list">...</div>
       <div id="story-list">...</div>

       <!-- Bottom filter strip (desktop: pinned bottom; mobile: inside filter expandable) -->
       <div id="filter-strip">
           <select id="filter-time">...</select>
           <button id="filter-drawer-toggle">Filters</button>
           <button id="search-toggle" aria-label="Search">&#128269;</button>
           <div id="search-expand">
               <input type="text" id="search-box" placeholder="Search by keyword, actor, or location..." />
               <button id="search-clear">&times;</button>
           </div>
       </div>

       <!-- Filter drawer (expands up from filter strip) -->
       <div id="filter-drawer">
           <div id="origin-section">...</div>
           <div id="source-section">...</div>
           <div id="concept-section">...</div>
           <label id="opinion-filter-label">...</label>  <!-- MOVED from filter-row -->
           <button id="btn-clear-all-filters">...</button>
       </div>
   </div>
   ```

4. **Remove `#search-row`** and `#filter-row` as separate elements (merged into `#filter-strip`).

### 5.2 CSS Changes (Summary)

**New rules needed:**
- `#mobile-bar` — floating bar styling (56px, flex, dark bg)
- `#drag-handle` — pill-shaped drag indicator
- `#filter-strip` — bottom-pinned filter bar
- `#search-toggle` — icon button with expand animation
- `#search-expand` — expandable search input container
- Mobile sidebar as bottom sheet (position: fixed, transform-based positioning)
- Bottom sheet snap states (`.sheet-closed`, `.sheet-half`, `.sheet-full`)
- Filter drawer upward expansion (`position: absolute; bottom: 100%`)

**Modified rules:**
- `#sidebar` mobile layout (remove width: 100%, max-height: 55vh; add fixed positioning)
- `#map-container` mobile (height: 100vh instead of 45vh)
- `#world-overview-bar` (remove `display: none !important` on mobile)
- `#worlds-bar` desktop (add `overflow-x: auto` and right-edge fade)

**Removed rules:**
- `#search-row` (element removed)
- `#filter-row` (element removed; functionality merged into `#filter-strip`)

**Light mode:** Every new element needs a `body.light-mode` counterpart. This includes:
- `#mobile-bar`, `#drag-handle`, `#filter-strip`, `#search-toggle`, `#search-expand`
- Bottom sheet background and border colors
- Search icon color states

### 5.3 JavaScript Changes (Summary)

**New functions:**
- `initBottomSheet()` — sets up touch listeners for swipe gestures on mobile
- `setSheetState(state)` — `'closed'` | `'half'` | `'full'`; sets CSS class + triggers map resize
- `toggleSearch()` — expands/collapses search input in filter strip
- `updateMobileBar()` — updates stats and world indicator in the floating bar

**Modified functions:**
- `toggleSidebar()` — on mobile, this becomes `setSheetState()` instead of CSS class toggle
- `applyFilters()` — call `updateMobileBar()` after filter application
- `switchWorld()` — call `updateMobileBar()` after world switch
- `updateStatsBar()` — also update `#mobile-bar-stats` text

**Touch handling for bottom sheet:**
```
touchstart -> record Y position
touchmove -> track delta Y, move sheet transform in real-time
touchend -> determine direction + velocity, snap to nearest state
```

This is a well-established pattern. Libraries like `react-bottom-sheet` exist but since we are vanilla JS, a ~50-line touch handler is sufficient.

### 5.4 What NOT to Change

- **The map rendering** — no changes to MapLibre, heatmap, dots, labels, country polygons
- **The info panel's function** — just its mobile presentation becomes a bottom sheet
- **The filter system logic** — `computeFilteredState()`, `applyFilters()`, all filter variables
- **The world system** — `WORLD_PRESETS`, `switchWorld()`, etc.
- **Space/Internet buttons** — position and behavior
- **Dark mode as default** — dark remains base, light is the override
- **Keyboard shortcuts** — all remain the same

### 5.5 Migration Considerations

- The `#search-row` and `#filter-row` elements are removed. Any JS that references these by ID needs updating to reference the new `#filter-strip` container.
- The `#opinion-filter-label` moves inside `#filter-drawer`. JS references to `document.getElementById("filter-opinion")` will still work since the element keeps its ID.
- The `#world-overview-bar` loses its `display: none !important` on mobile. It should now be visible in the bottom sheet.

---

## Part 6: Implementation Checklist for Builder

### HTML
- [ ] Add `#mobile-bar` element inside `#sidebar` (first child)
- [ ] Add `#drag-handle` element inside `#sidebar` (second child)
- [ ] Reorder sidebar children: move `#world-overview-bar` above `#worlds-bar`
- [ ] Reorder sidebar children: move `#view-toggles` above content lists
- [ ] Create `#filter-strip` element (replaces `#search-row` + `#filter-row`)
- [ ] Move `#opinion-filter-label` into `#filter-drawer`
- [ ] Add `#search-toggle` icon button and `#search-expand` container
- [ ] Update search box placeholder to "Search by keyword, actor, or location..."

### CSS
- [ ] Style `#mobile-bar` (56px floating bar, flex layout, hidden on desktop)
- [ ] Style `#drag-handle` (centered pill, hidden on desktop)
- [ ] Style `#filter-strip` (bottom-pinned, flex layout)
- [ ] Style `#search-toggle` and `#search-expand` (icon + expandable input)
- [ ] Mobile: `#sidebar` as fixed bottom sheet with transform states
- [ ] Mobile: `#map-container` at 100vh
- [ ] Mobile: Remove `display: none !important` from `#world-overview-bar`
- [ ] Mobile: bottom sheet snap state classes (`.sheet-closed`, `.sheet-half`, `.sheet-full`)
- [ ] Desktop: `#worlds-bar` overflow fix (`overflow-x: auto` + fade mask)
- [ ] Desktop: filter drawer expands upward from `#filter-strip`
- [ ] All light-mode counterparts for every new element
- [ ] Transition/animation for bottom sheet (cubic-bezier)

### JavaScript
- [ ] `initBottomSheet()` with touch handling (start, move, end)
- [ ] `setSheetState(state)` with CSS class management + map resize
- [ ] `toggleSearch()` expand/collapse animation
- [ ] `updateMobileBar()` to sync floating bar content
- [ ] Update `toggleSidebar()` for mobile branch
- [ ] Update `applyFilters()` to call `updateMobileBar()`
- [ ] Update `switchWorld()` to call `updateMobileBar()`
- [ ] Remove references to `#search-row`, `#filter-row`
- [ ] Wire up `#mobile-bar-menu` click handler

### Testing
- [ ] Dark mode: all new elements (mobile bar, drag handle, filter strip, search toggle)
- [ ] Light mode: all new elements
- [ ] Mobile: bottom sheet open/close/half/full states
- [ ] Mobile: swipe gestures (up/down from each state)
- [ ] Mobile: info panel as bottom sheet replacement + back navigation
- [ ] Mobile: map fills viewport when sheet is closed
- [ ] Mobile: map interaction works when sheet is half-open
- [ ] Desktop: sidebar order (overview above worlds, view toggles above content)
- [ ] Desktop: filter strip at bottom of sidebar
- [ ] Desktop: search expand/collapse
- [ ] Desktop: worlds bar with 4+ pills (no overflow)
- [ ] Desktop: filter drawer expands upward from bottom
- [ ] Tablet portrait (<=768px): mobile layout
- [ ] Tablet landscape (>768px): desktop layout
- [ ] Bump `?v=N` in `index.html`

---

## Part 7: Risk Assessment

| Risk | Severity | Mitigation |
|------|----------|------------|
| Touch gesture handling is tricky on older Android browsers | Medium | Use `passive: true` on touchstart/touchmove. Test on Chrome Android, Safari iOS, Firefox Android. Fall back to tap-to-toggle if gestures fail. |
| Bottom sheet conflicts with MapLibre's touch zoom/pan | Medium | Use `e.stopPropagation()` on the sheet element to prevent map from receiving touch events when the sheet is being dragged. Only intercept when touch starts on the drag handle or sheet header. |
| Filter drawer expanding upward may cover content the user was reading | Low | Add a subtle backdrop/dim behind the drawer. Clicking outside closes it. |
| Moving search to icon-triggered may confuse users who use "/" shortcut | Low | "/" shortcut should auto-expand the search field and focus it. |
| Large structural HTML changes may break Playwright tests | High | Many tests reference sidebar elements by ID. Since IDs are preserved (just reordered), most tests should still pass. New mobile-specific tests needed. |

---

## Part 8: Future Considerations

- **Gesture refinement:** The bottom sheet could support velocity-based flicking (fast swipe = skip intermediate state). This is polish, not MVP.
- **Mini-map mode:** On mobile half-sheet, the visible map portion could show a simplified overview. Not needed now.
- **Sidebar width customization on desktop:** A drag handle on the sidebar edge to resize. Low priority.
- **Persistent filter state in floating bar:** The floating bar could show active filter chips (e.g., "Sports | 24h"). Nice-to-have, not MVP.

---

## Appendix: Current vs New Sidebar Order Comparison

| Position | Current (Desktop) | New (Desktop) | Rationale |
|----------|-------------------|---------------|-----------|
| 1 | Header | Header | No change |
| 2 | Stats bar | Stats bar | No change |
| 3 | Search box | **World overview** | Most informative content, was buried at position 6 |
| 4 | Worlds bar | Worlds bar | No change |
| 5 | Time + Opinion + Filters | **View toggles** | Primary navigation, was at position 7 |
| 6 | **World overview** | Scrollable content | Main browsing area, moved up |
| 7 | **View toggles** | (bottom) Filter strip | Filters are secondary, moved to bottom |
| 8 | Scrollable content | | |

**Net effect:** The user sees the global summary and starts browsing situations/events immediately. Filters are one click away at the bottom but don't compete for attention.
